retail.label: Retail stores
retail.description: Walk-in / walk-out grocery and convenience retail. Optimize checkout throughput, shrink, labor coverage, refrigeration energy, and smart-store vision costs across footprints from kiosk to flagship.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Lower queueTime → higher queueEfficiency → more transactions × basket size in monthly revenue.
costDrivers.rationale: Walk-out shoppers tolerate ~45s before abandoning; the model scales conversion down as checkout seconds rise.
costDrivers.optimize: Reduce queue seconds to lift conversion and basket completion.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Higher peakLift amplifies dailyTransactions on top of baseVisitors for commuter burst windows.
costDrivers.rationale: 7–10 and 17–20 peaks carry disproportionate share of daily sales; missed peaks are lost same-day demand.
costDrivers.optimize: Staffing and promo timing for commuter peaks (7–10, 17–20).

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Shorter restockTime → higher restockEfficiency → more productsSold per transaction cycle.
costDrivers.rationale: Top SKUs stock out silently; slower replenishment caps sell-through even when footfall is strong.
costDrivers.optimize: Faster replenishment cycles reduce lost sales on top SKUs.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Lower exceptionRate × transactions × penaltyPerException directly cuts monthly exceptionPenalty.
costDrivers.rationale: Vision misses and walk-outs are pure margin leakage; each unresolved exception carries a fixed EUR penalty.
costDrivers.optimize: Vision AI and exception desk staffing cut walk-out losses.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: More staff lowers effective exceptionRate (mitigation) but raises staffingMonthlyCost in operatingCosts.
costDrivers.rationale: Floor coverage suppresses shrink but payroll is the largest variable opex line after fixed occupancy.
costDrivers.optimize: Balance floor coverage vs. payroll; ~1 FTE per 375 sq ft.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Lower energyComms subtracts directly from operatingCosts with no revenue trade-off in the model.
costDrivers.rationale: Open-deck refrigeration and HVAC often exceed 15% of utilities in grocery autonomous formats.
costDrivers.optimize: HVAC setpoints and night blinds on open decks.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Higher smartTechCost adds monthly opex; value comes indirectly via lower queueTime and exceptionRate in real ops.
costDrivers.rationale: Over-provisioned vision, gates, and edge compute erode margin on small-footprint stores.
costDrivers.optimize: Right-size vision, gates, and edge compute for format.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Larger footprintSqft raises baseFixedMonthlyCosts (sq ft × rate) but enables higher traffic and assortment scenarios.
costDrivers.rationale: Fixed rent and fit-out scale with area; formats below minimum sq ft carry a cost floor in the model.
costDrivers.optimize: Match selling area to catchment demand — avoid oversizing fixed occupancy.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Each downtime hour adds linear downtimePenalty as a % of monthly revenue (0h→0%, 12h→100%).
costDrivers.rationale: Gate or vision outages halt walk-out entirely; even short outages during peak destroy same-day net value.
costDrivers.optimize: Redundant edge nodes and proactive gate maintenance to stay near 0h downtime.

operationalNotes: Footprint scales fixed occupancy costs and suggests staffing benchmarks.
Queue friction is the primary conversion lever for autonomous checkout.
Cold + ambient energy typically dominates utilities in grocery formats.
Downtime penalty scales linearly from 0h to 12h (0%→100% of revenue).

pharmacy.label: Autonomous pharmacies
pharmacy.tagline: Regulated dispensing with minimal counter time
pharmacy.description: OTC plus prescription pickup with robotic dispensing and pharmacist verification. Optimize verification latency, Rx exceptions, consult minutes, cold chain, and formulary accuracy.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Lower queueTime → higher queueEfficiency → more completed Rx pickups and OTC attach per visitor.
costDrivers.rationale: Patients abandon pickup when verification queues exceed a few minutes; wait time replaces classic checkout friction.
costDrivers.optimize: Parallel verification queues and pre-pack staging cut wait.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Lower exceptionRate × transactions × penaltyPerException (EUR 28 each) cuts exceptionPenalty sharply.
costDrivers.rationale: Rx rework, DUR failures, and compliance fines cost far more per incident than grocery shrink.
costDrivers.optimize: Barcode validation and DUR checks reduce rework fines.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: More pharmacist staff mitigates exceptions faster but adds staffingMonthlyCost at EUR 185/day per FTE.
costDrivers.rationale: Mandatory consult minutes are non-deferrable; understaffing creates regulatory risk and queue spillover.
costDrivers.optimize: Tele-pharmacist coverage for off-peak consult demand.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Lower energyComms reduces operatingCosts; stable cold chain prevents stock write-offs modeled via exceptionRate.
costDrivers.rationale: Vaccine and biologic fridges require 24/7 monitoring; energy failures trigger mandatory product destruction.
costDrivers.optimize: Monitor vaccine fridges; alarm-linked redundancy.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Shorter restockTime → higher restockEfficiency → fewer out-of-stock scripts and lost refill revenue.
costDrivers.rationale: Generic PAR gaps cause patients to transfer; refill cycles are predictable and automatable.
costDrivers.optimize: Automated PAR levels for high-turn generics.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: smartTechCost is pure monthly opex; robots enable lower queueTime only when capacity matches script volume.
costDrivers.rationale: Dispensing robots sized for peak spike carry idle lease cost off-peak; right-sizing is the main ROI lever.
costDrivers.optimize: Match robot capacity to script volume, not peak spike.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: footprintSqft × EUR 32/sq ft (min EUR 6,200) sets baseFixedMonthlyCosts before automation and staff.
costDrivers.rationale: Pharmacy fixed costs are higher per sq ft than retail due to clean-room and cold storage requirements.
costDrivers.optimize: Compact layouts with robot storage density reduce occupancy without capping script capacity.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: downtimeHours scale linearly to downtimePenalty as % of monthly revenue — halts all autonomous pickup.
costDrivers.rationale: Robot or gate failure blocks every script; patients reroute to competitors within the same day.
costDrivers.optimize: Preventive maintenance windows off-peak; hot-swap robot modules.

operationalNotes: Higher basket value but lower traffic vs. grocery; exceptions are costlier.
Verification time replaces classic checkout queue in the model.
Staff mitigates exceptions more aggressively (pharmacist oversight).
Automation cost is modeled as monthly opex, not CAPEX amortization.

cinema_fb.label: Cinema F&B
cinema_fb.description: Lobby concessions and grab-and-go for cinema circuits. Optimize pre-show rush, order prep time, order accuracy, spoilage, and showtime-aligned labor.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Higher peakLift amplifies transactions in the 30–45 min pre-show window on top of baseVisitors.
costDrivers.rationale: Concession revenue clusters around showtimes; flat staffing during rush leaves demand uncaptured.
costDrivers.optimize: Stagger upsells and mobile pre-order to flatten peaks.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Lower queueTime → higher queueEfficiency → more guests served before auditorium doors close.
costDrivers.rationale: Guests who miss the trailer rarely return mid-film; handoff seconds directly cap conversion.
costDrivers.optimize: Batch popcorn/drink prep before trailer drop.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Shorter restockTime → higher restockEfficiency → faster prep-line turnaround between rushes.
costDrivers.rationale: Empty warmers during pre-show are lost upsell minutes; prep turnaround is the availability lever here.
costDrivers.optimize: Prep-line par levels tied to showtime schedule.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Lower exceptionRate × transactions × penaltyPerException (EUR 9) reduces spoilage and remake penalties.
costDrivers.rationale: Wrong combo orders become waste plus remake labor; accuracy is margin in high-spoil categories.
costDrivers.optimize: Kiosk confirmation screens reduce remakes and spoilage.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: More crew lowers queueTime indirectly in ops but raises staffingMonthlyCost at EUR 125/day per FTE.
costDrivers.rationale: Roster to seat count and show schedule, not average hourly traffic — peaks are binary per showtime.
costDrivers.optimize: Roster to seat count, not average hourly traffic.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Lower energyComms subtracts directly from operatingCosts between show cycles.
costDrivers.rationale: Popcorn warmers and compressors idle between shows; scheduling off-cycles saves without touching revenue levers.
costDrivers.optimize: Warmers off between cycles; idle compressor tuning.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: footprintSqft × EUR 22/sq ft (min EUR 4,800) drives baseFixedMonthlyCosts for lobby concession area.
costDrivers.rationale: Lobby depth limits queue depth; oversized footprint adds rent without adding pre-show throughput.
costDrivers.optimize: Design for queue depth and prep stations, not display area alone.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: downtimeHours apply linear downtimePenalty on monthly revenue — critical during pre-show windows.
costDrivers.rationale: Kiosk outage during a sold-out premiere is irrecoverable; guests buy nothing once seated.
costDrivers.optimize: Dual POS paths and offline kiosk fallback for peak shows.

operationalNotes: Peak uplift is typically higher than retail due to showtime clustering.
Restock lever models prep-station turnaround, not shelf facing.
Waste from wrong orders is captured in the exception rate penalty.
Mobile pre-order can be explored by lowering queueTime in scenarios.

bakery.label: Smart bakeries
bakery.tagline: Fresh production aligned to morning peaks
bakery.description: Smart bakery with bake-to-demand and grab-and-go. Optimize batch timing, display replenishment, freshness waste, morning peak capture, and production labor.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Shorter restockTime → higher restockEfficiency → fewer gaps between bake batches on display.
costDrivers.rationale: Mid-morning display gaps lose impulse purchases; batch timing is the bakery equivalent of shelf availability.
costDrivers.optimize: Demand forecasting cuts over-bake and midday gaps.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Higher peakLift amplifies dailyTransactions during 7–9am and lunch windows on baseVisitors.
costDrivers.rationale: Fresh-baked demand is time-boxed; missed morning peak cannot be recovered later in the day.
costDrivers.optimize: Pre-open staging and app pre-orders for 7–9am.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Lower queueTime → higher queueEfficiency → more combo purchases completed per peak hour.
costDrivers.rationale: Coffee + pastry combos need fast handoff; queue friction caps attach rate on high-frequency visits.
costDrivers.optimize: Self-checkout kiosks for coffee + pastry combos.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Lower exceptionRate × transactions × penaltyPerException (EUR 7) models less freshness waste and markdown.
costDrivers.rationale: Unsold fresh product is the primary margin leak; waste rate often exceeds retail shrink percentages.
costDrivers.optimize: Markdown automation and donation routing reduce write-offs.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: More bakers/front staff improve restockTime and exceptionRate in ops but raise staffingMonthlyCost.
costDrivers.rationale: Cross-trained staff cover morning peak and production; understaffing shows up as waste and empty display.
costDrivers.optimize: Cross-train bakers for front coverage in peaks.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: Lower energyComms subtracts from operatingCosts; smart scheduling avoids continuous full-bake mode.
costDrivers.rationale: Oven energy is a high fixed draw relative to bakery revenue, especially on smaller footprints.
costDrivers.optimize: Smart oven scheduling vs. continuous full bake.

costDrivers.benefit: Net value ↔ · forecast accuracy vs. opex
costDrivers.impactMechanism: smartTechCost adds monthly opex; ROI appears as lower exceptionRate and restockTime in operations.
costDrivers.rationale: Forecasting IoT pays off only when bake batches track demand curves, not when running full capacity blindly.
costDrivers.optimize: Link IoT spend to batch-size optimization, not hardware alone.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: footprintSqft × EUR 24/sq ft (min EUR 3,600) sets baseFixedMonthlyCosts for production + retail area.
costDrivers.rationale: Oven and display need minimum area; oversized footprint raises rent without proportional morning peak capture.
costDrivers.optimize: Right-size production cell to peak batch volume, not average day.

costDrivers.impactMechanism: downtimeHours scale linearly to downtimePenalty — oven or POS outage halts sales and production.
costDrivers.rationale: A down oven at 6:30am eliminates the highest-margin daypart entirely.
costDrivers.optimize: Redundant proofers and AM maintenance windows before open.

operationalNotes: Exception rate primarily models freshness waste and markdown loss.
Replenishment lever reflects display gaps between bake batches.
IoT forecasting spend is captured in smart tech monthly cost.
Smaller footprints still carry high oven energy relative to revenue.
